title: CrossCloudTransferRequested
description: Emitted by the SFTP watcher when a complete file is detected on the watch path. Consumed by the transfer worker, which copies the file to one or more cloud storage destinations.

schemaVersion.description: MAJOR.MINOR. MINOR bumps add optional fields only (non-breaking). MAJOR bumps may remove/rename/retype fields. Consumers MUST route events with an unsupported MAJOR to the DLQ with reason 'unsupported_schema_version'.

eventType.description: Discriminator for future event types on the same exchange/topic.

eventId.description: Unique ID for this specific publish attempt. Changes on republish; NOT used for de-duplication.

correlationId.description: Stable across the lifetime of a single file's journey (watcher -> queue -> consumer -> both uploads). Propagated into logs and DLQ messages for tracing.

idempotencyKey.description: Deterministic key derived from source path + file size + content checksum. Stable across redeliveries/retries of the SAME file, so the consumer can safely no-op on duplicates.

occurredAt.description: UTC timestamp (RFC 3339) when the watcher observed the file as stable/complete.

path.description: Absolute path of the file inside the watcher's mounted volume.

checksumSha256.description: SHA-256 of the file content at detection time. Used for idempotency key derivation and post-upload integrity verification.

key.description: Object key/path within the destination bucket.

destinations.description: One entry per cloud target. The consumer fans these out CONCURRENTLY and the event is only fully processed once every destination has ack'd.

retry.description: Populated by the consumer (not the watcher) when a message is republished after a transient failure. Absent on first publish.

lastError.description: Short machine-readable error code from the previous attempt, e.g. 'gcp-gcs:upload_timeout'.
